actors.description: Providence and Worcester Railroad Company is the target company; after the merger it would survive as a wholly owned subsidiary of G&W.
actors.exact_quote: Merger Sub will merge with and into the Company, with the Company being the surviving corporation and becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of G&W.
actors.label: Company / target

actors.description: Genesee & Wyoming Inc. (G&W) is the acquirer and ultimately winning bidder at $25.00 per share in cash.
actors.exact_quote: G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR
actors.label: G&W / acquirer

actors.description: Merger Sub is the G&W subsidiary merging into the Company.
actors.exact_quote: The merger agreement provides for the merger of Merger Sub with and into the Company at the effective time
actors.label: Merger Sub

actors.description: Party A was a Class I rail partner that initially suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed interest in acquiring equity in the Company.
actors.exact_quote: met with one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”) to discuss joint commercial opportunities. During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company
actors.label: Party A

actors.description: Party B was an unnamed potential strategic buyer that later became the principal competing finalist against G&W.
actors.exact_quote: the Company and representatives of GHF held an introductory meeting with another potential strategic buyer (“Party B”).
actors.label: Party B

actors.description: Party C was an unnamed potential strategic buyer not previously in the process; it approached management and later submitted an IOI and LOI.
actors.exact_quote: a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company.
actors.label: Party C

actors.description: Party D was an unnamed financial buyer that submitted an LOI and later revised its LOI upward, but did not proceed after the Company declined to commit not to sign with another bidder during Party D's diligence period.
actors.exact_quote: Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share (subject to a four-week diligence period).
actors.label: Party D

actors.description: Party E was an unnamed strategic buyer that submitted an LOI, revised it with financing support from Party F, then withdrew the revised proposal while confirming its original proposal.
actors.exact_quote: Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share (subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation).
actors.label: Party E

actors.description: Party F was an unnamed strategic buyer that submitted an LOI and later provided financing support for Party E's revised proposal.
actors.exact_quote: Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.20 per share (subject to a 30-day due diligence period).
actors.label: Party F

actors.description: GHF was initially engaged as financial advisor; its business was later acquired by BMO, which continued as financial advisor and delivered the fairness opinion.
actors.exact_quote: including Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC (the business of which was subsequently acquired by BMO Capital Markets Corp. and which we refer to as “GHF” prior to such acquisition and “BMO” thereafter).
actors.label: GHF / BMO

actors.description: Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP served as Company legal counsel in the process.
actors.exact_quote: the Company’s legal counsel, Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP (which we refer to as “Hinckley Allen”)
actors.label: Hinckley Allen

actors.description: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP served as G&W's legal counsel.
actors.exact_quote: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP (which we refer to as “Simpson Thacher”), G&W’s legal counsel
actors.label: Simpson Thacher

actors.description: The Board created a Transaction Committee of independent directors to supervise and oversee the sale process and make recommendations to the full Board.
actors.exact_quote: the Board appointed a special committee of independent directors of the Board (which we refer to as the “Transaction Committee”), composed of Frank W. Barrett (Chair), Richard W. Anderson and James C. Garvey, for the purpose of assisting in the supervision and oversight of the process on the full Board’s behalf and making a recommendation to the full Board.
actors.label: Transaction Committee

actors.description: The Eder Trusts were significant holders and entered into a voting agreement with G&W and the Company.
actors.exact_quote: The Eder Trusts have executed a voting agreement with G&W and the Company under which the Eder Trusts have agreed to vote their shares in favor of approving the merger agreement.
actors.label: Eder Trusts

actors.description: Director Alfred P. Smith recused himself from meetings concerning the bidding process because of potential conflict related to GATX, which owned 4.92% of common stock and might be a potential buyer.
actors.exact_quote: Director Smith is Vice President and Group Executive, Locomotive Group of GATX Corporation (“GATX”), which owns 4.92% of the Company’s common stock. In order to avoid any potential conflict, Mr. Smith elected to recuse himself from participating in this and other Board meetings at which the bidding process and any potential transaction were to be discussed because of the possibility that GATX might be a potential buyer of the Company.
actors.label: Director Smith / GATX

bids.description: The Company received nine written IOIs with prices from $17.93 to $26.50 per share, implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
bids.exact_quote: the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
bids.label: Initial IOIs

bids.description: Party C submitted an IOI at $21.00 per share, implying equity value of $108 million.
bids.exact_quote: On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million).
bids.label: Party C IOI

bids.description: The Company received six LOIs with prices ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 per share, implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million.
bids.exact_quote: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million).
bids.label: LOI round

bids.description: Party B submitted an LOI at $24.00 per share, subject to expedited diligence.
bids.exact_quote: Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review).
bids.label: Party B LOI

bids.description: G&W initially submitted an LOI at $21.15 per share, composed of $20.02 cash plus a $1.13 CVR tied to South Quay property sale proceeds, subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period.
bids.exact_quote: G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share (subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period). The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right, the value of which was based on the subsequent sale of the Company’s South Quay property (“CVR”).
bids.label: G&W initial LOI

bids.description: G&W revised its LOI upward to $22.15 per share, including $21.02 cash and $1.13 CVR, after feedback that its prior price and CVR structure were not competitive.
bids.exact_quote: On July 26, 2016, in response to feedback from representatives of GHF indicating its price and CVR structure were not competitive, G&W submitted a revised LOI, which increased its offer to $22.15 per share (including $21.02 in cash and $1.13 CVR).
bids.label: G&W revised LOI

bids.description: Party E submitted an LOI at $21.26 per share, subject to 60-day exclusivity for diligence and definitive documentation.
bids.exact_quote: Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share (subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation).
bids.label: Party E LOI

bids.description: Party D submitted an LOI at $21.00 per share, subject to a four-week diligence period.
bids.exact_quote: Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share (subject to a four-week diligence period).
bids.label: Party D LOI

bids.description: Party C submitted an LOI at $19.30 per share, subject to a 30-day diligence period.
bids.exact_quote: Party C submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.30 per share (subject to a 30-day diligence period).
bids.label: Party C LOI

bids.description: Party F submitted an LOI at $19.20 per share, subject to a 30-day due diligence period.
bids.exact_quote: Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.20 per share (subject to a 30-day due diligence period).
bids.label: Party F LOI

bids.description: Party D submitted a revised LOI at $24.00 per share with a 30-day diligence period.
bids.exact_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share
bids.label: Party D revised LOI

bids.description: Party E submitted a revised LOI at $23.81 per share with financing support from Party F and a 30-day diligence period.
bids.exact_quote: Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.
bids.label: Party E revised LOI

bids.description: Party E withdrew its revised $23.81 proposal but confirmed its original $21.26 per share proposal.
bids.exact_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share)
bids.label: Party E withdrawal of revised proposal

bids.description: Party D declined to shorten diligence and requested a no-signing commitment; after the Company declined, Party D would not proceed with further diligence then.
bids.exact_quote: Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.
bids.label: Party D did not proceed

bids.description: G&W submitted the winning revised LOI at $25.00 per share in cash, excluding the prior CVR, expiring at 6:00 p.m. the next day.
bids.exact_quote: G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and the voting agreement. G&W’s LOI further indicated that the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.
bids.label: G&W winning bid

bids.description: Party B declined to increase its offer after BMO contacted it at the Board's request.
bids.exact_quote: At the request of the Board, representatives of BMO then contacted Party B to determine if it would increase its offer price. Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.
bids.label: Party B no bump

bids.description: Board concluded G&W's offer was superior to Party B's, citing $1.00 higher per share price and lower termination fee, among other factors.
bids.exact_quote: The Board determined that the G&W offer represented the superior proposal.
bids.label: G&W superior proposal

chronology.description: The process began after Eder and Rogers met Party A to discuss joint commercial opportunities and Party A raised equity acquisition interest.
chronology.exact_quote: In the fourth quarter of 2015, Robert H. Eder ... and Frank K. Rogers ... met with one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”) to discuss joint commercial opportunities.
chronology.label: Initial Party A contact

chronology.description: At a regular quarterly Board executive session, the subcommittee recommended retaining GHF, and the Board approved the engagement.
chronology.exact_quote: During the executive session of the Board’s regular quarterly meeting held on January 27, 2016 ... the subcommittee recommended to the Board that the Company retain GHF as its investment banking firm.
chronology.label: GHF retained

chronology.description: The Board approved pursuing discussions with Party A and soliciting other potential buyers, and created the Transaction Committee.
chronology.exact_quote: Representatives of GHF then proposed a process pursuant to which the Company would proceed with discussions with Party A and also solicit interest from other potential third parties regarding a change in control transaction.
chronology.label: Sale process authorized

chronology.description: Company management met with Party A representatives.
chronology.exact_quote: On March 22, 2016 and March 23, 2016, Daniel T. Noreck ... and other members of the senior management team of the Company met with various representatives of Party A.
chronology.label: Party A meetings

chronology.description: Transaction Committee authorized GHF to contact Party A and other potential buyers regarding interest in a change in control transaction.
chronology.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee authorized GHF to contact Party A as well as other potential buyers to determine their level of interest in a change in control transaction.
chronology.label: Buyer outreach authorization

chronology.description: GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers including Party A and 18 potential financial buyers.
chronology.exact_quote: During the week of March 28, 2016, in accordance with the Transaction Committee’s directives, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers.
chronology.label: Initial outreach

chronology.description: GHF and Company management held introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers, including G&W, at the 2016 Connections Convention.
chronology.exact_quote: Between April 3, 2016 and April 6, 2016, representatives of GHF and members of the Company’s management held a series of introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers (including G&W) at the American Short Line and Regional Railroad Association’s 2016 Connections Convention
chronology.label: Convention meetings

chronology.description: Transaction Committee authorized GHF to confirm continued interest from convention parties, other strategic buyers including Class I railroads, and private equity firms under confidentiality agreements.
chronology.exact_quote: the Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF, on behalf of the Company, to contact each of the five interested parties with whom management had met at the 2016 Connections Convention, as well as other potential strategic buyers (including certain Class I railroads) and private equity firms who had entered into confidentiality agreements with the Company to confirm their continuing interest.
chronology.label: Continued outreach

chronology.description: The Company and GHF circulated an information memorandum to confidentiality-agreement parties; IOIs were requested by May 10, later extended to May 19.
chronology.exact_quote: each potential buyer had been advised to submit a non-binding indication of interest by May 10, 2016 (which deadline was subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016 to ensure the potential buyers had sufficient time to formulate their most compelling offers).
chronology.label: IOI deadline set

chronology.description: Transaction Committee reviewed nine IOIs and excluded the two low bidders from management presentations; seven bidders advanced to management presentations and diligence.
chronology.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee ... concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process. The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence.
chronology.label: Seven bidders advanced

chronology.description: GHF instructed potential buyers to submit LOIs by July 20, 2016 with markups of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement.
chronology.exact_quote: representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement
chronology.label: LOI instructions

chronology.description: Draft merger agreement, voting agreement for Eder Trust shares, and disclosure letter were posted to the data site.
chronology.exact_quote: On June 29, 2016, the Company posted an initial draft of merger agreement to the internet data site, and on June 30, 2016, the Company posted an initial draft of voting agreement with respect to the common stock and preferred stock held by the Eder Trusts. On July 11, 2016, the Company posted an initial draft of disclosure letter to the internet data site.
chronology.label: Draft documents posted

chronology.description: Potential buyers and advisors conducted due diligence and met management.
chronology.exact_quote: Over the course of June and July 2016, each of the potential buyers and their respective advisors engaged in due diligence of the Company’s business and financial condition, including holding meetings with certain members of the Company’s management team.
chronology.label: Diligence phase

chronology.description: Transaction Committee chose to proceed with confirmatory diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B because of their higher offers; other bidders were told they were no longer in the process.
chronology.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee concluded that the Company should proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B because of the higher offers made by each of G&W and Party B relative to the other potential buyers. At the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF subsequently contacted the remaining bidders to inform them that they were no longer involved in the process.
chronology.label: Finalists selected

chronology.description: After Party D and Party E reengaged with higher proposals, the Transaction Committee considered them but ultimately directed work to continue with G&W and Party B, prioritizing Party B because of its higher price.
chronology.exact_quote: the Transaction Committee directed the management team and representatives of each of BMO and Hinckley Allen to continue diligence and negotiations with both G&W and Party B, but given the higher price offered by Party B, to prioritize Party B and proceed to complete the negotiation of the merger agreement with Party B.
chronology.label: Party B prioritized

chronology.description: Party B and G&W conducted on-site financial, legal and environmental due diligence, including hi-rail track inspections.
chronology.exact_quote: From July 27, 2016 through August 11, 2016, Party B and G&W conducted on-site financial, legal and environmental due diligence, including hi-rail trips to inspect the Company’s track infrastructure.
chronology.label: Finalist diligence

chronology.description: Transaction Committee directed BMO to tell G&W that the Company intended to sign with another bidder, but G&W was permitted to continue diligence to facilitate a possible improved offer.
chronology.exact_quote: the Transaction Committee directed representatives of BMO to inform G&W that the Company intended to enter into a merger agreement with another bidder. G&W requested that it be allowed to continue its due diligence ... to potentially facilitate an improved offer, which request was granted.
chronology.label: G&W given chance to improve

chronology.description: G&W submitted a revised $25.00 all-cash LOI; the Transaction Committee told Party B another party had submitted a higher bid.
chronology.exact_quote: Following that discussion, the Transaction Committee directed representatives of BMO to inform Party B that another party had submitted a revised LOI at a higher price.
chronology.label: Higher G&W bid communicated

chronology.description: The Board met, compared Party B and G&W drafts and regulatory paths, determined regulatory timing and likelihood were not materially different, and selected G&W after Party B refused to increase.
chronology.exact_quote: Based upon that discussion, the Board determined that the timeframe for, and the likelihood of, obtaining the required regulatory approval was not materially different for a transaction with G&W versus a transaction with Party B.
chronology.label: Board selection

chronology.description: BMO delivered an oral fairness opinion, later confirmed in writing, that the merger consideration was fair from a financial point of view to common holders other than the Eder Trusts.
chronology.exact_quote: BMO then rendered an oral opinion to the Board, subsequently confirmed by delivery of a written opinion, dated August 12, 2016
chronology.label: Fairness opinion

chronology.description: Board approved the merger agreement and recommended shareholder approval; Director Smith recused himself.
chronology.exact_quote: Following a discussion of the terms of the merger agreement and related matters, the Board (other than Director Smith, who recused himself from the August 12, 2016 meeting) (i) determined that the merger agreement, the plan of merger and the merger and other transactions contemplated by the merger agreement are fair to, advisable and in the best interests of the Company and its shareholders
chronology.label: Board approval

chronology.description: The Company publicly announced the transaction before market open.
chronology.exact_quote: The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016 prior to the opening of trading of the Company’s common stock on the NASDAQ.
chronology.label: Announcement

chronology.description: Board considered later-disclosed BMO affiliate participation in G&W's debt facility and determined it was not material to BMO's independence.
chronology.exact_quote: On September 6, 2016, the Board considered this new information, determined this relationship was not material to BMO’s independence and confirmed that the Board remained comfortable with the engagement and opinion of BMO.
chronology.label: BMO relationship review

counts.description: GHF contacted 29 potential buyers total: 11 strategic and 18 financial.
counts.exact_quote: representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers.
counts.label: Initial contacted parties

counts.description: All 11 strategic buyers and 14 financial buyers executed confidentiality agreements, totaling 25 parties under NDA.
counts.exact_quote: Each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements.
counts.label: Confidentiality agreements

counts.description: Introductory meetings were held with five potential strategic buyers at the 2016 Connections Convention, including G&W.
counts.exact_quote: held a series of introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers (including G&W)
counts.label: Convention meetings count

counts.exact_quote: the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers
counts.label: IOI count

counts.description: Two low bidders were excluded; seven potential buyers advanced to management presentations and additional diligence.
counts.exact_quote: the two low bidders should be excluded from that process... schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers
counts.label: Advanced bidders count

counts.exact_quote: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs
counts.label: LOI count

counts.description: Three bidders—Party B, G&W and Party D—provided markups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement; Party E provided a summary of issues and proposed changes.
counts.exact_quote: Party B, G&W and another bidder (“Party D”) also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement. One bidder (“Party E”) provided a summary of material issues and proposed changes
counts.label: Markup count

counts.description: One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.
counts.exact_quote: One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.
counts.label: No-LOI parties

counts.description: Two finalists, G&W and Party B, were selected for confirmatory diligence and negotiations.
counts.exact_quote: proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B
counts.label: Finalists count

counts.description: The Board cited 25 confidentiality-agreement parties and six LOI submitters as part of the breadth of the process.
counts.exact_quote: 25 of whom entered into confidentiality agreements with the Company and received information related to the Company and six of whom submitted non-binding letters of intent
counts.label: Board process count

counts.description: Management and affiliates held 1,107,002 common shares and 500 preferred shares, representing approximately 22.7% of common and 78.1% of preferred.
counts.exact_quote: the directors and executive officers of the Company and their affiliates collectively beneficially owned 1,107,002 shares of common stock ... and 500 shares of preferred stock, or approximately 22.7% of the outstanding common stock and approximately 78.1% of the outstanding preferred stock.
counts.label: Management ownership

counts.description: Eder Trusts held 842,742 common shares and 500 preferred shares, representing approximately 17.3% of common and 78.1% of preferred.
counts.exact_quote: the Eder Trusts held 842,742 shares of common stock, representing approximately 17.3% of the outstanding shares of common stock, and 500 shares of preferred stock, representing approximately 78.1% of the outstanding shares of preferred stock.
counts.label: Voting agreement shares

relations.description: GHF represented it would not represent a buyer or financing source in connection with an offer to buy the Company.
relations.exact_quote: GHF represented to the Company that it would not represent any potential buyer of the Company in connection with an offer to purchase the Company or any person proposing to provide financing in connection with such an offer.
relations.label: GHF conflict representation

relations.description: The Transaction Committee did not have authority to approve or enter into a change of control transaction; that authority remained with the full Board under Rhode Island law.
relations.exact_quote: The Transaction Committee did not have the authority to approve or enter into a change of control transaction, which, under Rhode Island law, is an authority that can only be exercised by the full Board.
relations.label: Committee authority limitation

relations.description: Confidentiality agreements did not contain standstill provisions restricting proposals or offers.
relations.exact_quote: The confidentiality agreements did not contain any “standstill” or similar provision that would prohibit a potential buyer from submitting a proposal or making an offer to acquire the Company.
relations.label: No standstill

relations.description: The draft voting agreement concerned common and preferred stock held by the Eder Trusts.
relations.exact_quote: the Company posted an initial draft of voting agreement with respect to the common stock and preferred stock held by the Eder Trusts.
relations.label: Eder Trust voting agreement draft

relations.description: Executed voting agreement obligated Eder Trusts to vote in favor of merger agreement approval.
relations.exact_quote: The Eder Trusts have executed a voting agreement with G&W and the Company under which the Eder Trusts have agreed to vote their shares in favor of approving the merger agreement.
relations.label: Support agreement

relations.description: Directors and executive officers were expected to vote for the proposals, but only Mr. Eder was obligated to do so.
relations.exact_quote: We currently expect that each of these individuals will vote all of his or her shares of common stock and preferred stock in favor of each of the proposals to be presented at the special meeting, although none of them other than Mr. Eder are obligated to do so.
relations.label: Management voting expectation

relations.description: Both Party B and G&W draft merger agreements contained no-solicit provisions subject to fiduciary out for unsolicited superior proposals.
relations.exact_quote: both Party B’s and G&W’s draft merger agreements restricted the Company from soliciting a competing proposal, subject to a “fiduciary out” for an unsolicited superior proposal
relations.label: No-shop / fiduciary out

relations.description: G&W termination fee was 3% of transaction value, negotiated down from 3.85%; Party B fee was 3% plus expenses capped at 1%.
relations.exact_quote: a termination fee equal to 3% of the transaction value in the case of G&W (which the Company had negotiated down from 3.85%) and 3% plus Party B’s transaction expenses (capped at 1% of transaction value) in the case of Party B
relations.label: Termination fee comparison

relations.description: Final merger agreement permitted the Company before shareholder approval to negotiate certain unsolicited bona fide takeover proposals and terminate for a superior proposal, subject to notice, matching rights, and $3.785 million termination fee.
relations.exact_quote: the Board is permitted to terminate the merger agreement in order to enter into a definitive agreement for that superior proposal, subject to certain notice requirements, “matching rights” and payment of a termination fee of $3.785 million to G&W
relations.label: Superior proposal rights

relations.description: The merger agreement had no financing condition; Board cited G&W's financial wherewithal.
relations.exact_quote: the absence of a financing condition in the merger agreement and the representation of G&W in the merger agreement as to its financial wherewithal
relations.label: No financing condition

relations.description: STB approval or exemption was a closing condition; alternative path allowed closing into a voting trust if STB had provided an informal opinion that the voting trust insulated G&W from unauthorized control.
relations.exact_quote: either (i) final approval or authorization of, or exemption by, the STB with respect to the merger has been obtained ... or (ii) if the STB has not issued a decision ... then the STB has provided G&W with a written informal opinion that the proposed voting trust submitted by G&W to the STB would insulate G&W from unauthorized control of the Company
relations.label: STB / voting trust condition

relations.description: G&W agreed to seek STB approval to close into a voting trust if necessary to reduce regulatory-delay risk to Company shareholders.
relations.exact_quote: the agreement of G&W in the merger agreement to seek STB approval to close into a voting trust, if necessary, to ensure that the merger can be completed as promptly as possible and ensure that the risk of regulatory delays is not borne by the Company or its shareholders
relations.label: Voting trust covenant

relations.description: Shareholder approval required majority of outstanding common and preferred shares, each voting separately as a class.
relations.exact_quote: holders of at least a majority of the outstanding shares of common stock and preferred stock, each voting separately as a class, have approved the merger agreement and the plan of merger
relations.label: Approval threshold

relations.description: BMO confirmed no existing or contemplated material financial advisory or financing services relationships with G&W, but later identified an affiliate's participation in G&W's debt facility.
relations.exact_quote: BMO confirmed that it did not have any existing or contemplated material relationships with G&W in connection with the provision of any financial advisory or financing services to G&W.
relations.label: BMO G&W relationship confirmation

relations.description: BMO affiliate was a lender with approximately 5% participation in G&W's senior secured syndicated facility and had been paid $1 million to $5 million in fees and interest since January 1, 2014.
relations.exact_quote: an affiliate of BMO is a lender with an approximately 5% participation in the syndicate and for which such affiliate has been paid revenue (fees and interest) between $1 million and $5 million, in the aggregate, since January 1, 2014.
relations.label: BMO affiliate debt participation

relations.description: BMO fee for financial advisory services was approximately $2.52 million, with majority payable on consummation.
relations.exact_quote: the Company will pay BMO an aggregate fee of approximately $2.52 million ... the remainder and majority of which is payable upon consummation of the merger.
relations.label: BMO fee

source_limitations: The source window is a proxy statement excerpt and provides anonymized bidder identities for Parties A through F rather than actual names, except G&W and GATX references.
The excerpt does not include the complete merger agreement termination section; it cuts off mid-list after 'by the Company if prior to the approval'.
The source presents a sale process from the Company/Board perspective, not a tender offer process; no tender offer mechanics are described.
The window includes the background, Board reasons, fairness opinion summary, and part of merger agreement summary, but not all annexes or full confidentiality agreement/voting agreement text.

uncertain_or_missing.description: Actual identities of Parties A, B, C, D, E, and F are not disclosed in the source excerpt.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”)
uncertain_or_missing.label: Anonymous bidders

uncertain_or_missing.description: The excerpt states GATX might be a potential buyer, but does not say GATX actually submitted a proposal or participated in the process.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: because of the possibility that GATX might be a potential buyer of the Company.
uncertain_or_missing.label: GATX potential buyer status

uncertain_or_missing.description: The source states one strategic buyer and one financial buyer did not submit LOIs but does not identify them.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.
uncertain_or_missing.label: Non-LOI bidders unidentified

uncertain_or_missing.description: The source does not specify the two low IOI bidders excluded from management presentations.
uncertain_or_missing.exact_quote: the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process.
uncertain_or_missing.label: Excluded IOI bidders unidentified
